associatedMedia.headline: Land in Isle of Wight, Virginia

memoryArtifacts.headline: Land in Isle of Wight, Virginia
The area of Lawne's Creek, James River, Surry County, Virginia
memoryArtifacts.description: In 1624, Thomas Bennett Jr. and his wife, Agnes Beard Bennett, lived on the south side of the James River, near Lawne's Creek. This is around the time that their children, John Bennett and Philip Bennett, were born.

memoryArtifacts.headline: Mouth of the Chickahominy River, James River, Virginia
Governor's Land, James River, Virginia
memoryArtifacts.description:
In the sixteenth and early 
seventeenth century this area was the center of a 
Paspahegh Indian village, and contained their burial
 ground.  When Jamestown colony was founded, a plantation was established here in 1619.  The area now contains a country club and golf course.  Artifacts from the area are on display at the country club.

nameMeanings.name: Bennett
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from the medieval personal name Benedict , from Latin Benedictus ‘blessed’. In the 12th century the Latin form of the name is found in England alongside versions derived from the Old French form Beneit, Benoit, which was common among the Normans. The surname has also been established in Ireland since the 14th century.
German: from a short form of the personal name   Bernhard  .
Altered form of French Benet or Bennet or of some other similar (like-sounding) French surname. Altered ending reflects the Canadian and American French practice of sounding the final -t.

variants.name: Bennetts
meanings: English (Cornwall): variant of Bennett , with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: Dittus
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Gerhard, Fritz, Kurt, Otto, Reinhold.
German: from a vernacular short form of the Latin personal name Benedictus (see Bennett ).

variants.name: Benedict
meanings: English, German, and Dutch: from the personal name Benedict, from Latin Benedictus ‘blessed’. This owed its popularity in the Middle Ages chiefly to Saint Benedict of Norcia (c. 480–550), who founded the Benedictine order of monks at Monte Cassino and wrote a monastic rule that formed a model for all subsequent rules. No doubt the meaning of the Latin word also contributed to its popularity as a personal name, especially in Romance countries. Occasionally the English surname may derive from Latin benedicite ‘bless (you)’, perhaps given as a nickname to an habitual user of the expression. In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed the German variant Benedikt and many cognates from other languages, e.g. Hungarian Benedek , Slovenian Benedik (see Benedick ), and also their patronymics and other derivatives, e.g. Italian Benedetti .

variants.name: Bennight
meanings: Probably an altered form of English   Bennett  .

variants.name: Bennison
meanings: English: patronymic from the Middle English personal name Bene(i)t + -son. See Bennett and compare Benson .

nameMeanings.name: Thomas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament name, borne by one of Christ's twelve apostles, referred to as ‘Thomas, called Didymus’ (John 11:16; 20:24). Didymos is the Greek word for ‘twin’, and the name is the Greek form of an Aramaic byname meaning ‘twin’. The given name has always been popular throughout Christendom, in part because St Thomas's doubts have made him seem a very human character.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

en.lifeSummary: Thomas Bennett was born in England as the son of Thomas Bennett and Not Anstice is proven. He married Agnes Beard on 17 July 1623, in Wiveliscombe, Somerset, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 1 daughter. He died on 23 December 1668, in London, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Thomas Bennett est né à Angleterre, fils de Thomas Bennett et Not Anstice is proven. Il a épousé Agnes Beard le 17 juillet 1623, à Wiveliscombe, Somerset, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé le 23 décembre 1668, à Londres, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Thomas Bennett wurde in England, als Sohn von Thomas Bennett und Not Anstice is proven geboren. Er hat am 17. Juli 1623 in Wiveliscombe, Somerset, England, United Kingdom Agnes Beard geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Er starb am 23. Dezember 1668 in London, England.

it.lifeSummary: Thomas Bennett è nato il nel England come figlio di Thomas Bennett e Not Anstice is proven. Lui sposato Agnes Beard il 17 luglio 1623, nel Wiveliscombe, Somerset, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 1 figlia. Morì il 23 dicembre 1668, nel London, England.

ko.lifeSummary: Thomas Bennett(은)는 영국에서 Thomas Bennett(와)과 Not Anstice is proven의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 1623년 7월 17일 영국 잉글랜드 서머세트 위벨리스코브에서 Agnes Beard(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1668년 12월 23일 영국 런던에서 사망했다.

Ele casou-se com Agnes Beard em 17 de julho de 1623, em Wiveliscombe, Somerset, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 1 filha. Ele faleceu em 23 de dezembro de 1668, em London, Inglaterra.
